root_motivation: Land the smallest filesystem-substrate that makes the laptop's five reflective tools (daily-receipt, confidant, letters-we-never-sent, conversations-zine, memory-reliquary) composable across the daily→weekly→monthly→quarterly→annual pyramid, without rewriting any of them. Substrate-only: tier-wiring belongs to follow-on bind PRDs.
user_persona: jsy on the wintermute laptop, where five reflective-artifact tools already exist as Rust binaries under ~/wintermute/ but produce isolated outputs that no upstream consumer can enumerate by tier. The cadence CLI is used by both the user (manual record/list/latest) and by extended versions of the five tools (bind PRDs that shell out to `cadence record`).

acceptance_criteria.description: cargo install --path . installs binary `cadence` to ~/.local/bin/; `cadence --version` reports 0.1.0.
`cadence where` on a fresh laptop creates ~/.claude/cadence/ and reports counts of 0 across all tiers.
`cadence register daily-receipt --tier daily` records the tool in manifest.json under tools[].name == 'daily-receipt'.

`cadence record daily --produced-by daily-receipt --path /tmp/test.escpos --summary 'manual test'` creates a record file at ~/.claude/cadence/daily/$(date +%Y-%m-%d)/<ulid>.json and prints the ulid to stdout.
`cadence list daily --json | jq -r '.[].id'` includes the ulid from AC4.
`cadence latest daily --produced-by daily-receipt --json | jq -r '.path'` returns the path from AC4.
`cadence list daily --since 7d --json` returns only records whose produced_at is within the last 7 days.
`cadence record weekly --produced-by confidant --path /tmp/w.md --sources <ulid>` correctly records sources: ['<ulid>'] and the file lands under ~/.claude/cadence/weekly/$(date +%Y-W%V)/<ulid>.json.
cargo test --release green; lib + integration suite.
`cadence record` is append-only — two records on the same day by the same tool both persist; `latest` returns the newer one.
Repo has a CHANGELOG.md with a ## v0.1.0 section enumerating the four primary subcommands and the directory schema.

scope: Subcommands: register, record, list, latest, where.
Filesystem substrate at $CADENCE_HOME (default ~/.claude/cadence/) with daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly/annual buckets.
JSON record schema with ulid id, tier, period, produced_by, produced_at, path, sources, summary, meta.
Period derivation: daily=YYYY-MM-DD, weekly=YYYY-Www (ISO 8601), monthly=YYYY-MM, quarterly=YYYY-Q[1-4], annual=YYYY.
manifest.json tracking registered tools and tier defaults.
non_goals: Tier-wiring of the five reflective tools (deferred to bind PRDs).
Indexing or full-text search over summary fields.
Garbage collection or pruning of old records.
Network egress: substrate is purely local filesystem, no HTTP.

five_whys_trace.a: Five reflective-artifact tools exist on this laptop and produce time-bucketed outputs, but no consumer can enumerate 'what dailies do I have this week' to feed the weekly tier.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does that matter — the tools are individually useful.
five_whys_trace.a: The pyramid only earns its name if a higher tier composes from the lower; otherwise it's five independent tools sharing aesthetic, not architecture.

five_whys_trace.q: Why not have each tool track its own siblings?
five_whys_trace.a: Per-tool tracking pushes coupling into five places and forces five version bumps for any schema change; a shared substrate centralizes it once.

five_whys_trace.q: Why a CLI rather than a library?
five_whys_trace.a: Each tool is already its own binary with its own dependency tree; shell-out to `cadence record …` keeps the substrate decoupled and the bind PRDs trivial (one new dep: nothing).

ambiguities_resolved.question: PRD lists `register` in the CLI but doesn't enumerate it among the 'four primary subcommands' in AC11.
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Treat register as setup-time and document the four primary verbs (record, list, latest, where) plus register as a setup helper in CHANGELOG.

ambiguities_resolved.question: PRD says crate name `cadence` collides with one published Rust crate.
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: publish = false in Cargo.toml; local install via cargo install --path . only. Publishing to crates.io is out of scope.
